The '''BC-714 luxury transport''', also known as the '''InterGalactic Banking Clan Starship''' or '''InterGalactic Banking Clan ship''', was a sleek [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] designed by the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] for the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]]. As the BC-714 was one of the fastest [[Starship/Legends|starships]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the Banking Clan used it as a transport vessel for its [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] executives to travel to [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] with which they had business dealings. The BC-714 had a luxurious interior, which often led to its passengers despising less comfortable vessels.

Banking Clan [[Chairman/Legends|Chairman]] [[San Hill/Legends|San Hill]] used a BC-714 luxury transport as his personal starship. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] convoy led by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Subjugator-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Subjugator''-class heavy cruiser]] [[Malevolence/Legends|''Malevolence'']], contained a pair of BC-714s.

==Characteristics==
The BC-714 luxury [[Transport/Legends|transport]] was one of the fastest and most comfortable transport [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Smooth, lean, and [[Missile/Legends|rocket]]-shaped, the 55.2-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long BC-714 was designed with aerodynamic flight in mind. The transport contained [[Twin ion engine/Legends|twin ion engines]], which provided enormous thrust both in [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] and out. The [[Ion engine/Legends|thrusters]] enabled it to reach an atmospheric speed of 1,800 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|km/h]]. The ship was also equipped with a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] and an advanced [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], which had a Class 1 rating, as well as a backup hyperdrive with a Class 6 rating. Two variations of the ship existed, one equipped with twin [[Sublight engine/Legends|engines]],<ref name="TCWCG" /> and another with four.<ref name="Card" />

[[File:Bc_transports_Malevolence.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Two BC-714 luxury transports join General Grievous's flagship.]]
A pleasure [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] at heart, the BC-714 offered many amenities for the wealthy, such as luxurious staterooms for passengers, a twenty-seat [[Holotheater/Legends|holotheater]], and a dining room that rivaled the finest [[Restaurant/Legends|restaurants]] on [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]]. Seventeen crew members were needed to fly the ship on a normal voyage, with provided quarters for thirty passengers. Although unarmed, the BC-714 compensated for its lack of weaponry with sheer speed and heavy [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], which made it ideal for the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] executives of the [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who used the ship as a symbol of their status.<ref name="TCWCG" />

The vessel had a cargo capacity of up to eighty [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] and could carry consumables for up to six [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. Usually, the ship did not carry any additional complement vessels. A new BC-714 luxury transport was priced at 1.7 million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], while a used one cost 980,000 credits. The ship's availability was restricted to members of the [[Galaxy/Legends|InterGalactic]] Banking Clan.<ref name="TCWCG" />

==History==
The BC-714 luxury transport was designed and produced by the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] on behalf of the InterGalactic Banking Clan.<ref name="TCWCG" /> The ship was also referred to as the "InterGalactic Banking Clan Starship" due to its use by the finance conglomerate for [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] business travel.<ref name="Card" /> The transport was used as a luxury vessel by the Muun executives of the Banking Clan and gathered much prestige. The BC-714 was so comfortable that most passengers despised other, less luxurious vessels after traveling on it.<ref name="TCWCG" />

[[San Hill/Legends|San Hill]], the Banking Clan's [[Chairman/Legends|chairman]], used a BC-714 as his personal starship during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]].<ref name="SanHill">{{DB|character|sanhill|Hill, San}}</ref> In the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a pair of BC-714 luxury transports served in a Separatist [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] led by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] [[Subjugator-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Subjugator''-class]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Malevolence/Legends|''Malevolence'']].<ref name="TCWCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|For the Commerce and Banking Clan ships, George wanted designs that harkened back to 1950s-style rocket ships.|Marc Gabbana|<ref name="Art">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode II: Attack of the Clones]]''</ref>}}
[[File:IGBC_ship_concept.jpg|thumb|right|125px|InterGalactic Banking Clan ship concept art]]
The BC-714 luxury transport was developed by [[Marc Gabbana]]<ref name="SWcom">{{SW|url=news/back-from-the-drawing-board-part-2-repurposed-star-wars-technology|text=Repurposed ''Star Wars'' Technology - Back from the Drawing Board, Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> for [[George Lucas]]'s [[2002]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], as a starship for the InterGalactic Banking Clan.<ref name="IGBC" /> Lucas requested a retro-style design for the Banking Clan and [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] starships, similar to rocket ships seen in [[Wikipedia:List of science fiction films of the 1950s|1950s science-fiction films]]. When Gabbana created the Banking Clan ship, he designed it to be capable of vertical takeoffs and landings. Since the first concept art looked too sleek, the artist bulked up the starship to fit them into the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[universe]]. In the end he added a dark opening near the ship's top to break up its cylindrical form.<ref name="Art" />

The [https://web.archive.org/web/20100604013132/http://starwars.com/databank/organization/igbc/index.html InterGalactic Banking Clan entry] in the original [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] stated that the starships are not seen clearly in the finished film.<ref name="IGBC" /> According to [[Kevin Beentjes]]'s StarWars.com blog entry [http://www.starwars.com/news/back-from-the-drawing-board-part-2-repurposed-star-wars-technology "Back from the Drawing Board, Part 2: Repurposed ''Star Wars'' Technology]," the starship does not appear in the film at all.<ref name="SWcom" /> The BC-714's name was first mentioned in the [[2009]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement book ''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]''.<ref name="TCWCG" /> The ship's card in the [[Attack of the Clones (TCG)|''Attack of the Clones'']] set of the ''[[Star Wars Trading Card Game]]'' depicts it with four ion [[Engine/Legends|thrusters]]<ref name="Card" /> instead of the twin-ion engine mentioned in ''The Clone Wars Campaign Guide''.<ref name="TCWCG" />
